jokes.text: What if we let off some fireworks in here, huh? Fireworks in the bar? It's never been done before. Right?
jokes.setup: Charlie suggests fireworks indoors for a business meeting
jokes.punchline: The complete absurdity presented as innovative business thinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - dangerous idea presented as business innovation. Sets tone for their incompetence.

jokes.text: If it's a guy, I got a football ready for him... What if it's a guy who hates football but loves flowers? Or a woman who loves football but hates flowers?
jokes.setup: Simple gender-based gift preparation
jokes.punchline: Overthinking leads to increasingly complex scenarios
jokes.explanation: Classic overthinking escalation, mildly funny but predictable setup for the gang's neuroses.

jokes.text: Or a woman who was assigned male gender at birth but has retained her traditional love of the game? Yes. Which she has the right to do. That is her right... Of course, it's 2021... That's fine.
jokes.setup: Attempting to be progressive and inclusive
jokes.punchline: Awkward, performative wokeness that reveals their actual ignorance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe comedy - characters trying too hard to be progressive while clearly not understanding the issues.

jokes.text: Here's the bottom line, you guys: it's 2021, all bets are off, okay? The chances of this person walking in and being your typical vanilla white guy are slim to none. Hi. Sorry to interrupt. Uh, my name's Gary.
jokes.setup: Dennis confidently predicts diversity
jokes.punchline: Immediately proven wrong by the most vanilla white guy possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect comic timing and misdirection. Dennis's overconfident prediction immediately undermined by reality.

jokes.text: Oh, thank sweet Jesus. All right! Gary. This is a relief. It is so good to see you. This I can wrap my head around.
jokes.setup: Their previous anxiety about diversity
jokes.punchline: Uncomfortable relief at encountering a white man named Gary
jokes.explanation: Dark character comedy revealing their prejudices through supposedly 'harmless' relief.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm so sorry. Uh, there must be some confusion. I'm not here to hand out PPP loans. I'm here to collect on ones you already have.
jokes.setup: They assume Gary is there to give them money
jokes.punchline: He's actually there to investigate their fraud
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom misdirection that sets up the entire episode's premise effectively.

jokes.text: Hey, when the world says slow, we say go. That's right. Or strike. Or attack.
jokes.setup: Trying to sound entrepreneurial
jokes.punchline: Escalates to violent language inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Good character-based escalation showing their tendency toward aggression.

jokes.text: As an out-of-work bouncer, I was looking to pursue my other interests that would be as intellectually stimulating as choking out a jabroni and leaving him out in the street. Like my longtime dream of becoming an international country teen pop sensation.
jokes.setup: Mac wants intellectual stimulation
jokes.punchline: Compares violence to country teen pop as equally intellectual pursuits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac delusion - equating mindless violence with artistic aspiration. Pure character comedy gold.

jokes.text: ♪ Girl, you give me feels when I see them heels / Let's ride on a horse, making love out in the fields / Never mind them flies ♪
jokes.setup: Mac's 'country teen pop' song
jokes.punchline: Terrible lyrics combining romance with flies and horses
jokes.explanation: Mac's complete lack of musical talent perfectly executed through awful lyrics.

jokes.text: It feels like I'm asking her to forget the flies that are surrounding me, not the horse.
jokes.setup: Dennis critiquing Mac's lyrics about flies
jokes.punchline: Dennis seriously analyzing terrible lyrics as if they could be improved
jokes.explanation: Dennis's pretentious analysis of Mac's terrible song shows his need to appear sophisticated.

jokes.text: ♪ Never mind them thighs ♪ Thighs? Where What? Why thighs? Yeah, well, maybe he's got thick, grotesque thighs and he's embarrassed about 'em.
jokes.setup: Mac changes 'flies' to 'thighs' in his song
jokes.punchline: Dennis creates an elaborate backstory about grotesque thighs
jokes.explanation: Good absurd escalation but not as strong as other moments in the episode.

jokes.text: I can't stop thinking about the election... Our democracy is at stake... we were concerned that our guy wasn't gonna get a fair shot
jokes.setup: They sound like concerned patriots worried about election integrity
jokes.punchline: Setting up for the reveal that they're talking about Kanye
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the Kanye reveal, but relies on the payoff later for full impact.

jokes.text: Do I want a bunch of eggheads who spend all day learning about shit influencing how I think about things? No.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining why he doesn't trust experts
jokes.punchline: Anti-intellectual stance delivered by someone clearly lacking intelligence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac character moment - anti-intellectual sentiment from someone proving the point.

jokes.text: Look at this one, with the New York hat. She got bussed in from that liberal hellscape?
jokes.setup: Mac sees someone in a New York hat
jokes.punchline: Immediately assumes conspiracy theory about bussed-in voters
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mac's conspiracy mindset and prejudices.

jokes.text: We're gonna ask you a series of Philly-related questions in no particular order... Who is Uncle Eddie Savitz? Who is a better athlete, Donovan McNabb or Rocky Balboa?
jokes.setup: Creating a test to verify Philadelphia residency
jokes.punchline: Comparing real athlete to fictional character as if it's a legitimate question
jokes.explanation: Great absurd logic - treating fictional character as real in their 'test' perfectly shows their warped thinking.

jokes.text: Rocky Balboa is a fictional character, so this isn't even a conversation. Are you saying you want Sly Stallone to be the starting QB for the Eagles next season?
jokes.setup: Woman points out Rocky is fictional
jokes.punchline: Mac doubles down with even more absurd logic about Stallone as quarterback
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Mac's inability to admit he's wrong, leading to increasingly absurd arguments.

jokes.text: So was Doug Flutie. You saying he's an actor? No, I'm say— He's one of the greatest football players that Canada ever produced.
jokes.setup: Comparing Stallone's height to Doug Flutie
jokes.punchline: Mac's random assertion about Flutie being Canadian
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment but getting repetitive in the escalation.

jokes.text: Now you're comparing McNabb to a teeny-tiny little man who's, uh, named after a woodwind instrument?
jokes.setup: Discussion about Doug Flutie
jokes.punchline: Mac's observation that 'Flutie' sounds like 'flute'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that fits Mac's character but isn't particularly strong.

jokes.text: Hey, what's up, Brian? Good to see you again... You want us back at the hundred feet? We'll go back to the hundred.
jokes.setup: They're acting like poll watchers
jokes.punchline: Reveals they've been kicked back multiple times and have a restraining distance
jokes.explanation: Great reveal that they're repeat offenders who've been progressively pushed back from the polling place.

jokes.text: ♪ Freedom is the choice you have / Is it Rocky or McNabb? / Cast your vote and make it clear ♪
jokes.setup: They're now busking with a voting theme
jokes.punchline: Their song is about their earlier ridiculous debate
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows how their minds work - turning their argument into a song.

jokes.text: The labeling was a bit obtuse and created a great deal of confusion... we might have been entirely responsible for the vote delays
jokes.setup: Mac admits their voting boxes caused problems
jokes.punchline: Casually admits they may have disrupted the entire election
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark escalation - casual admission of potentially derailing democracy delivered with complete nonchalance.

jokes.text: What were you importing? Inks, grease, paste. Sludges in general.
jokes.setup: Gary asks about Frank's import business
jokes.punchline: Frank's business is importing various gross substances
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank business - dealing in disgusting substances presented matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: And, uh, what were you exporting? Hair dye.
jokes.setup: Frank imports sludges
jokes.punchline: Exports hair dye, setting up his Giuliani story
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the Giuliani reveal, economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: When society hits the brakes, I hit the gas, baby. Society break, I got gas.
jokes.setup: Frank's philosophy during the pandemic
jokes.punchline: Mangled metaphor that accidentally becomes about flatulence
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank - trying to sound profound but accidentally making a fart joke.

jokes.text: If you have gas, take it out in the hall, okay?
jokes.setup: Frank's accidental gas reference
jokes.punchline: Charlie takes it literally
jokes.explanation: Simple callback but works for Charlie's literal-minded character.

jokes.text: You look crazy, dude. Where you going? I'm out.
jokes.setup: Frank has a terrible half-done hair dye job
jokes.punchline: Frank leaves mid-dye job looking ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Good visual setup for Frank's appearance later, character-appropriate impatience.

jokes.text: Why does nobody old know how to wear a mask?
jokes.setup: Frank struggling with his mask
jokes.punchline: Charlie's exasperated observation about old people and masks
jokes.explanation: Relatable pandemic observation delivered through Charlie's perspective.

jokes.text: Fortunately, my favorite bookstore never closes, and they never judge their customers.
jokes.setup: Frank talking about his favorite bookstore
jokes.punchline: It's clearly a porn shop, not a bookstore
jokes.explanation: Good Frank euphemism - calling a porn shop his 'favorite bookstore' with typical Frank logic.

jokes.text: There I was, staring at my idols, Peter North, T.T. Boy, the late, great D.J. Ram.
jokes.setup: Frank describing his 'reading material'
jokes.punchline: Lists porn stars as his idols with reverent tone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank moment - treating porn stars with the reverence others reserve for historical figures.

jokes.text: Shoe polish, India ink, permanent marker. I tried 'em all. Until I finally hit pay dirt. Chinese motor oil.
jokes.setup: Frank experimenting with hair dye ingredients
jokes.punchline: Escalates to using motor oil as hair dye
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Frank's complete disregard for safety and logic in pursuit of his goals.

jokes.text: Little did I know, my work was about to be in the national spotlight, on the head of America's mayor, Rudolph William Giuliani.
jokes.setup: Frank's mysterious client buildup
jokes.punchline: Reveals he did Giuliani's infamous melting hair dye
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff connecting Frank's ridiculous hair dye business to a real historical moment. Perfect blend of absurdity and current events.

jokes.text: Funny thing about the spotlight, though, sometimes it burns too bright. And that was the moment I knew it was coming to an end.
jokes.setup: Frank reflecting on his Giuliani success
jokes.punchline: Dramatic language about the spotlight 'burning' while literally talking about melting hair dye
jokes.explanation: Clever double meaning - the 'burning' spotlight literally melted his hair dye creation.

jokes.text: Wait, so you're saying that the hair dye that's dripping down your face is the same dye used by Rudolph Giuliani during the presidential election? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Gary's incredulous question about the hair dye
jokes.punchline: Frank's casual 'Yeah' while presumably looking ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Great visual payoff with Gary's disbelief contrasted with Frank's nonchalant confirmation.

jokes.text: Charlie and I, we're the last tit on the hog before the asshole.
jokes.setup: Dee trying to describe their work relationship
jokes.punchline: Incredibly crude and nonsensical metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awful Dee metaphor that's both crude and makes no anatomical sense.

jokes.text: I am so sorry for my partner's blue language. It does not represent our business, and it's extremely off-putting. It was just a joke. Just stop.
jokes.setup: Charlie apologizing for Dee's crude language
jokes.punchline: His formal, corporate tone is completely out of character for Charlie
jokes.explanation: Funny character reversal - Charlie being the professional one. The formal language contrasts with his usual chaos.

jokes.text: That's a mask. Yeah, of Ronald Reagan, but... Ronald Reagan? That's Dennis.
jokes.setup: Charlie showing off his mask creation
jokes.punchline: Made a Dennis mask thinking it was Ronald Reagan
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Charlie's confusion about faces and Dennis's vanity in being mistaken for Reagan.

jokes.text: 'Fauch the Grouch.' Right? 'Mama needs wine.' Right? And this is funny 'cause women are alcoholics who hate their kids.
jokes.setup: Charlie showing off his 'funny' mask designs
jokes.punchline: Explains the misogynistic 'joke' behind the mask with complete sincerity
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy through Charlie's innocent delivery of horrible stereotypes as if they're clever observations.

jokes.text: Halloween store owners, they're gonna be, like, some of the richest people in America, Dee.
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining his business logic
jokes.punchline: Believes Halloween store owners will become the richest people in America
jokes.explanation: Good Charlie logic - wildly optimistic and specific prediction based on flawed reasoning.

jokes.text: Get the pelts, you bitch! What pelts? The pelts! The pelts, you bitch! You insufferable bitch.
jokes.setup: Charlie and Dee working under stress
jokes.punchline: Charlie's increasing profanity and obsession with raccoon pelts
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Charlie's stress-induced aggression and his weird relationship with animals.

jokes.text: The pandemic had kind of disrupted the bar's natural ecosystem. There was a real uptick in raccoons, which consider rat brains a delicacy.
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining the raccoon situation
jokes.punchline: Treats the bar like a nature preserve with its own ecosystem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie worldview - treating a filthy bar as if it's a nature preserve with its own delicate ecosystem.

jokes.text: Gentlemen. You got the stuff? Absolutely. Give me one minute. Hey. Digging that face paint. Very festive. What are you gentlemen celebrating?
jokes.setup: Meeting customers who want costumes
jokes.punchline: Complimenting their 'face paint' not realizing it's for something sinister
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy setup - their innocent compliment about face paint before learning it's for insurrection.

jokes.text: You're telling me that you provided the iconic costumes at the Capitol riots? Yeah. Yep. Can't say they weren't good costumes, Gary.
jokes.setup: Gary realizes they helped the Capitol rioters
jokes.punchline: They're proud of their craftsmanship despite the context
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - being proud of their work quality while completely missing the moral implications.

jokes.text: [Firework goes off in the bar] Fire. Oh! Huh? What? Shit, I see it.
jokes.setup: Callback to Charlie's opening fireworks idea
jokes.punchline: The firework actually goes off and starts a fire
jokes.explanation: Good callback payoff - Charlie's terrible idea from the opening finally happens with predictable results.

jokes.text: You people have shamelessly gamed the system out of massive amounts of taxpayer dollars for fictitious businesses that have gone bankrupt, and still you have the nerve to ask for more money?
jokes.setup: Gary's outraged summary of their fraud
jokes.punchline: Sets up their response about it being 'the American way'
jokes.explanation: Good setup for their defense, Gary serves as the straight man effectively.

jokes.text: Well, that's what makes America the greatest country in the world. When you're in need, the government comes in and bails you out. Nothing made me more money than businesses I sent into bankruptcy.
jokes.setup: Defending their fraud
jokes.punchline: Treating taxpayer bailouts as the essence of American greatness
jokes.explanation: Good dark satire of American capitalism through the gang's twisted logic.

jokes.text: 'Washington outsider, not taken seriously by the liberal media, successful businessman, unsuccessful in love, electrifying onstage.' Oh, yeah. That's our guy. That's Kanye. Kanye West.
jokes.setup: Gary assumes they're talking about Trump based on their description
jokes.punchline: Reveals they were talking about Kanye West the whole time
jokes.explanation: Brilliant long-form misdirection that recontextualizes the entire episode. Perfect comedy writing that subverts expectations masterfully.

jokes.text: I do not believe any of you. None of this happened. There is no way that you were all involved in every major event of the past year. Why? Because you're not Forrest Gump!
jokes.setup: Gary's mounting disbelief at their stories
jokes.punchline: Forrest Gump reference calling out the absurdity of their involvement in everything
jokes.explanation: Great meta moment where a character calls out the show's absurd premise directly.

jokes.text: Stop the count! Stop the count! [Playing over Kanye West music]
jokes.setup: They're listening to Kanye to cheer up
jokes.punchline: Charlie chants 'Stop the count' while Kanye plays, showing confused political allegiances
jokes.explanation: Good ironic ending - chanting Trump slogans while celebrating their Kanye support shows their confused political identity.
